commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The PR needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer-visible upgrade/security handling before merge; ClawSweeper should not request repair markers while the external proof gate is still mock-only.; Needs attention: The patch changes the command-execution approval boundary and can silently ignore an existing stricter approval policy on upgrade.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve existing approval policy bef... (sha=2126197adabf6eed2df1edeb6d1a2926c201bb79)
commands.review_summary:
This PR moves exec approval file/socket defaults to `resolveStateDir()`, extracts state-dir and approval-surface leaf contracts, and adds state-dir/symlink regression tests.

PR surface: Source +184, Tests +102. Total +286 across 26 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main resolves exec approvals with `expandHomePrefix("~/.openclaw/...")` while the documented state-dir override is handled by `resolveStateDir()` elsewhere. I did not run a filesystem repro because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor real-behavior proof and maintainer-visible upgrade/security handling before merge; ClawSweeper should not request repair markers while the external proof gate is still mock-only.

target.title: fix(exec-approvals): resolve path/socket from the state dir (honor OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR)
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `exec-approvals.{json,sock}` were resolved home-relative (`expandHomePrefix("~/.openclaw/…")`), **ignoring `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`** — the one subsystem that didn't honor the documented state-dir override. On a relocated-state install this wrote approvals to `$HOME/.openclaw` (wrong location), and if `$HOME/.openclaw` was a symlink to the relocated dir, the exec-approvals symlink guard refused to traverse it and **every exec tool-call failed** (`Refusing to traverse symlink in exec approvals path`).
- CI also exposed static import-cycle fallout around approval/config/channel contracts; this update extracts the affected leaf contracts without changing approval policy or channel behavior.

Why does this matter now?

- Relocated state dirs are a documented, supported configuration (`OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`). Today they silently split exec-approvals state, or hard-fail exec entirely with a symlinked home.
- The branch must also keep `build-artifacts` and runtime topology architecture checks green before review.

What is the intended outcome?

- exec-approvals resolve from `resolveStateDir()` — the same resolver `device-auth-store`, `restart-sentinel`, and `push-web` already use — so they honor `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` and fall back to `<home>/.openclaw`.
- Approval reply rendering remains a formatter; channel-specific approval availability/setup is resolved outside the reply formatter and passed through approval-unavailable details.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No change to approval policy, command analysis, or the socket protocol. Config-supplied `socket.path` overrides are untouched.
- No change to channel approval capabilities beyond moving type-only/runtime lookup boundaries to leaf contracts.

What does success look like?

- Default installs: byte-for-byte the same paths (`<home>/.openclaw/exec-approvals.*`). Relocated installs: approvals live in `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and a symlinked-home setup no longer fails exec.
- Import-cycle guards report zero cycles, and `build:ci-artifacts` completes.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The invariant: **approvals live in the state dir; the symlink guard's trusted root is the state dir's parent.** When `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` is unset, `dirname(<home>/.openclaw) === <home>` — i.e. the guard root equals the previous `resolveRequiredHomeDir()` value, so behavior is identical for all existing installs. The change only affects relocated-state installs, where it (a) puts approvals in the right place and (b) *keeps* the `.openclaw`-component symlink check instead of skipping it via `allowOutsideRoot`.
- The leaf-contract boundaries in `src/infra/state-dir.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approval-surface.types.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-message.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/current-inbound-prompt-context.types.ts`, and `src/channels/plugins/bundled-entry-contract.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #90971

Related: `resolveStateDir()` consumers `src/infra/device-auth-store.ts`, `src/infra/restart-sentinel.ts`, `src/infra/push-web.ts`. Documented precedence: https://docs.openclaw.ai/help/environment/ ("Explicit path variables such as `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` … still take precedence").

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Yes — owner.

## Files touched

- State-dir and exec approvals: `src/infra/state-dir.ts`, `src/config/paths.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approvals.ts`
- Approval surface/reply contracts: `src/infra/exec-approval-surface.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approval-surface.types.ts`, `src/infra/exec-approval-reply.ts`, related tests
- Exec host approval flow: `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-message.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-request.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-node.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`, `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts`
- Prompt/queue leaf contracts: `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/current-inbound-prompt-context.types.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/params.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.ts`, `src/agents/cli-runner/types.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/prompt-prelude.ts`, `src/auto-reply/reply/queue/types.ts`
- Bundled channel entry leaf contract: `src/channels/plugins/bundled.ts`, `src/channels/plugins/bundled-entry-contract.ts`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: exec-approvals path resolution under `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, including a symlinked-home relocated install; CI failures in `build-artifacts` and runtime topology architecture checks.
- Real environment tested: macOS local checkout, Node 24.16.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```
  node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts
  node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts
  node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-surface.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-request.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts
  ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>
  git diff --check
  pnpm build:ci-artifacts
  ```

- Evidence after fix (copied live output):

  ```
  Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
  Madge import cycle check: 0 cycle(s).
  [test] passed 4 Vitest shards in 13.54s
  All matched files use the correct format.
  pnpm build:ci-artifacts completed successfully, including build:plugin-sdk:dts, check-plugin-sdk-exports, and ui:build.
  ```

- Observed result after fix: approvals resolve to the configured state dir; approval reply guidance remains available through the approval-unavailable detail path; both import-cycle guards and the build-artifacts gate pass locally.
- What was not tested: a full multi-host gateway/live approval run.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: local macOS proof only; GitHub CI will re-run on the pushed PR head.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node --import tsx scripts/check-import-cycles.ts` → `0 runtime value cycle(s)`
- `node --import tsx scripts/check-madge-import-cycles.ts` → `0 cycle(s)`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.security-floor.test.ts src/config/paths.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-reply.test.ts src/infra/exec-approval-surface.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-shared.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-approval-request.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/runtime-context-prompt.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.tools.test.ts` → 4 shards, 9 files, 188 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 <touched files>` → clean
- `git diff --check` → clean
- `pnpm build:ci-artifacts` → passed

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- New `src/infra/exec-approvals-state-dir.test.ts`: (1) HOME-only → `<home>/.openclaw`; (2) `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` precedence over the HOME default (and explicitly *not* the HOME path); (3) a relocated state dir whose `.openclaw` final component is a symlink is **refused** by the guard.
- Updated approval surface/reply tests to keep channel-specific native approval guidance while avoiding a static reply-to-surface import edge.
- Updated exec-host shared tests and embedded tool-result handling to carry native approval setup details through approval-unavailable details.
- Existing symlinked-home store tests pass unchanged (confirming the no-regression invariant).

What failed before this fix, if known?

- On a relocated install with a symlinked `$HOME/.openclaw`, every exec tool-call failed with `Refusing to traverse symlink in exec approvals path`; approvals were otherwise written to `$HOME/.openclaw` rather than the configured state dir.
- CI failed `build-artifacts` and runtime topology architecture checks on the PR branch before the leaf-contract cleanup.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — tests added and updated.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Yes for relocated-state installs (approvals now in `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, exec no longer fails with a symlinked home). No for default installs — paths are identical.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- Behavior now honors `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` for exec-approvals. Existing default-install files are unaffected. Note: an already-relocated install that previously wrote approvals to `$HOME/.openclaw` will, after this change, read/create them in `$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` (call out in release notes if relevant).

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

- This is the exec-approvals (command-execution authorization) path. The symlink guard is preserved and, for relocated installs, strengthened (the `.openclaw` component is now checked instead of skipped). The file-level symlink-write refusal is unchanged. No change to approval policy or the socket protocol.

What is the highest-risk area?

- The symlink guard's trusted-root change.
- The approval/config/channel import-boundary cleanup needed to keep architecture checks green.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Invariant: `dirname(<home>/.openclaw) === <home>`, so for every non-`STATE_DIR` install the guard root is unchanged (byte-for-byte prior behavior). The relocated case gains a check it previously skipped. Covered by the symlinked-home store tests (unchanged) plus a new relocated-symlinked-`.openclaw` refusal test.
- Both import-cycle guards and `pnpm build:ci-artifacts` pass after the leaf-contract cleanup.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- CI and maintainer review.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- GitHub CI on the updated PR head.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- CI failures addressed: `build-artifacts`, runtime topology architecture checks.
